§6Compressor§f

§aPressurize fluids and gases for use in advanced refining and pneumatic systems!§f

The §bCompressor§f is a §e3×9×4§f multiblock machine that compresses liquids and gases, measured in Pressure Units (PU). It is most commonly used alongside the §bVacuum Refinery§f, which requires crude oil pressurized to §e2 PU§f. Each operation takes §e5 seconds§f and consumes §e2.5 kHE/t§f.

§6Crafting§f (Assembly Factory):
§b• §e8×§f Cast Steel Plate
§b• §e4×§f Copper Plate
§b• §e2×§f Steel Shell
§b• §e3×§f Motor
§b• §e1×§f Analog Circuit Board

§6Key Recipes§f:
§b• Crude Oil§f → §e2 PU§f (requires two compressors for vacuum refinery feed).
§b• Petroleum Gas§f (1 PU) → §bLiquid Petroleum Gas§f.
§b• Perfluoromethyl§f (1 PU) → §bCold Perfluoromethyl§f.
§b• Blood§f (3 PU) → §bHeavy Oil§f.
§b• Air§f → compressed air for §bPneumatic Tubes§f.

§6Compact Compressor§f:
A smaller §e3×3×7§f variant with identical functionality, crafted with:
§b• §e8×§f Cast Steel Plate
§b• §e4×§f Titanium Shell
§b• §e4×§f Copper Pipe
§b• §e2×§f Motor
§b• §e4×§f Integrated Circuit Board

§eTip§f: You will need at least §e2§f compressors running simultaneously to feed a Vacuum Refinery. Use the Compact Compressor variant for space-saving vertical stacking!
